frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: parietal neurons accumulating evidence about motion direction

frameworks.name: decision confidence / choice certainty
frameworks.one_line: same accumulator signal also encodes graded certainty

frameworks.name: post-decision wagering (opt-out paradigm)
frameworks.one_line: behavioral readout of certainty via safe sure-bet option

core_question: Do neurons that form a perceptual decision also represent the decision-maker's confidence in it?
key_assumptions: confidence is a graded, probabilistic assessment of expected outcome
opt-out choices reveal the animal's internal certainty
parietal neurons previously linked to decision formation are candidate substrates
manipulations: direction-of-motion difficulty across a range of coherences
trial-by-trial availability of an opt-out for small certain reward
key_findings: monkeys used the opt-out option in a graded way reflecting their certainty
parietal neurons represented formation of the direction decision
the same neurons encoded the degree of certainty underlying opt-out choices
interpretations: a single parietal population jointly represents the decision and confidence in it
decision confidence is computed within the perceptual-decision circuit, not by a separate module
